source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As part of an exercise program, a \( 75\, \text{kg} \) person does toe raises in which he raises his entire body weight on the ball of one foot. The Achilles tendon pulls straight upward on the heel bone of his foot. This tendon is \( 25\, \text{cm} \) long and has a cross-sectional area of \( 78\, \text{mm}^2 \) and a Young’s modulus of \( 1470\, \text{MPa} \). By how many millimeters does the exercise stretch his Achilles tendon?

image_dependency: requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is registered as image-reliant
image_manifest contains 1 existing image and registry_passed=true
the text provides tendon material properties but does not explicitly provide all statics/geometry details needed to determine tendon force, suggesting the missing setup likely comes from the figure
multi_step: solving requires more than direct substitution: first infer the tendon force from the body-weight/toe-raise setup, then apply Young's modulus deformation relation to compute stretch
the problem combines mechanics/statics context with material deformation quantities (length, area, Young's modulus)
numeric answer is not stated in the text and must be derived through chained calculations
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with a concise numeric result ('4.4mm')
choices are provided, enabling easy final-answer checking against multiple-choice options
the expected solution uses standard physics formulas, so intermediate steps and final units can be checked mechanically

risk_flags: image-required geometry/setup is not explicit in the question text alone
unit normalization may matter when verifying ('4.4mm' vs '4.4 mm')

decision_reasons: question, answer, and the required image asset are all present, so the core semantics are recoverable
registry passed with no recorded asset issues
initial scoring is strong across image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
